caseStudy.title: CRM Data Quality Overhaul
caseStudy.description: Cleaning and enriching a 2M-record customer database.
caseStudy.overview: A B2B enterprise had accumulated years of duplicated, incomplete, and outdated records in its CRM, undermining sales team confidence in the data and making marketing segmentation unreliable. The project aimed to establish a trusted, enriched customer database as the foundation for growth.
caseStudy.problem: An estimated 34% of CRM records contained duplicate entries, and 41% were missing critical firmographic data. Sales reps spent an average of 45 minutes per day manually verifying contact information before outreach.
caseStudy.solution: An automated data quality pipeline was built to deduplicate records using probabilistic matching, enrich missing fields via third-party data providers, and flag stale contacts for review. Ongoing data health monitoring was implemented with automated alerts when quality scores dropped below threshold.

results.summary: Sales teams now operate from a trusted data foundation, improving outreach effectiveness and pipeline predictability. Marketing campaigns benefit from reliable segmentation, improving ROI on demand generation spend.
